name: Homemade Cheddar Cheese
recipeInstructions:
1. Measure 1 gallon of milk into an 8-quart pot.
2. Stir in the buttermilk, cover the pot, and let the mixture stand at room temperature for at least 4 hours, or overnight.
3. Slowly bring the milk mixture to 86 °F over hot water using a double boiler method described in the 'cottage cheese' recipe.
4. Add cheese color if desired.
5. Slowly raise the milk temperature to 88–90 °F.
6. Add the rennet tablet mixed with ¼ cup of cool water; stir to blend.
7. Remove from heat and cover. Let stand until the curd is firm, breaking clean over your finger as described in the 'pot cheese' recipe. This should take about 30–40 minutes.
8. Cut the firm curds into ½‑inch pieces by slicing across vertically and diagonally with a long-bladed knife.
9. Gently stir the curds for 15 minutes using long, slow sweeping movements; avoid rapid stirring that will break the curds.
10. If any large curds remain, cut them into ½‑inch pieces.
11. Gradually raise the curd temperature to 100 °F, stirring gently every 3–5 minutes to prevent breaking the curd but keep it from sticking together.
12. Keep stirring until the curds reach a firm consistency that feels like well-formed scrambled eggs with individual pieces but not rubbery. This may take between 30 and 60 minutes.
13. If the curds do not become firm, increase the temperature gradually by about 10 °F above your target range while stirring more frequently; avoid scalding as this will make the cheese mushy.
14. When properly firm, remove from heat and allow to stand in the whey for 1 hour or until they toughen somewhat, stirring every 5–10 minutes.
15. Scoop off as much whey as possible. Pour curds into cheesecloth lined with a colander, tilt gently to drain off whey (the curd may be firm enough at this point to add salt and serve).
